response:
<<Sorting by Length with Grace>>

```sql
SELECT title, director, LENGTH(title) AS title_length, LENGTH(director) AS director_length
FROM movies
ORDER BY 
    title_length DESC,
    director_length DESC,
    title COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci,
    director COLLATE utf8mb4_unicode_ci;
```

This SQL query organizes records from the `movies` table by sorting the `title` and `director` columns in descending order based on their character length. In cases where two titles or directors have the same length, they are further sorted alphabetically to ensure a consistent and deterministic order.

A palindrome in this response is: **"madam"**.
